items.title: 0 Button Mechanist Power 29.7k Realistic
items.rotation: Set your mech skills to auto cast (ctrl + right click). That's it.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity engineer build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic Conditions = 10 conditions on golem. This build scales with conditions on foe (modified ammunition).

0:00 Rotation
2:34 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, the build is pretty simple. It is zero-button. The only thing you want to make sure you do is control-right-click all of your mech skills so the mech can play itself. You don't have to input any of those skills. For the notable rifle skills, skill 4 is a CC and skill 5 is a decent amount of mobility. If you're out of position, you can use skill 5 to get back to your team and away from danger. If you want to increase the build to one button, you can just spam Blunderbuss. It is worse than running sword, which is the official one-button build that I have, but you can use it. It does a little less damage, but it has the advantage of rifle and being fully ranged.

For notable engineer utility skills, Healing Turret is a decent amount of AoE healing and condi cleanse when you overcharge it and pop it. You probably want to leave your Shift Signet and Force Signet as last-resort tools because using them results in a pretty substantial DPS loss. Shift Signet will break stun and teleport you, which is useful on fights where you'll die otherwise if you're in a bad position. Force Signet is a 15% damage increase passively, so you don't really want to use it unless you're about to die, though it is a CC if you do pop it. Your elite will bring your mech back to life if it dies, so you probably just never want to swap it off.

If you're going to swap out any of the utilities, you probably want to swap out the condition damage signet. Some useful alternatives are Barrier Signet for a projectile-blocking dome, Rocket Boots for mobility, Personal Battering Ram for CC, and Throw Mine for boon strip and CC. Throw Mine is especially useful in fractals if something has No Pain, No Gain. Engineer has a whole bunch of other utility that I kind of look at as weapon-swap-ish, so I don't really recommend them because the whole point of the build is to have no weapon swapping. Those are probably the most notable ones. Thank you for watching and I hope you enjoy the build.

items.title: 1 Button Mechanist Power 34.3k Realistic
items.rotation:
Set your mech skills to auto cast (ctrl + right click).

Spam sword 2.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity engineer build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic Conditions = 10 conditions on golem. This build scales with conditions on foe (modified ammunition).

0:00 Rotation
2:21 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the one-button rundown, it's pretty simple. All you really want to do is spam Sword 2. Finishing your auto-attack chains reduces the cooldown on Sword 2, so basically every two auto-attack chains you'll have Sword 2 up, and once you have it up, you use it. You want to make sure that you set your mech skills to autocast by holding Control and right-clicking all of your mech skills.

On sword/shield, Sword 3 is a little bit of mobility. Shield 4 is a reflect, and if you press it again it does CC. Skill 5 is also a CC, so you can basically use both of these whenever there's a break bar. Healing Turret, if you pop it, is a condition cleanse and some healing, so you can run that instead of Rectifier Signet if you wish.

Your elite will re-summon your mech if it dies, so I would probably just recommend running it and popping it if your mech dies. Shift Signet and Force Signet you probably don't want to use, but in the worst-case scenario you can use them to get around. Shift Signet will teleport you, and Force Signet will CC. Using both of these skills results in a DPS loss, though, so try to save them if you can. If you're going to replace any of your utility skills, you want to replace your condi signet. Some notable alternatives are Barrier Signet as a projectile destruction skill, Rocket Boots for mobility, Personal Battering Ram for CC, and Throw Mine as both boon strip and CC.

Engineer has a lot of kits and gadgets that I kind of consider weapon-swap-ish, which is why you avoid using them in these builds. Those are the most relevant ones I'd point out on Engineer. Thank you for watching, and I hope you enjoy the one-button build.

items.title: 2 Button Mechanist Condi 35.7k Realistic
items.rotation:
Set your mech skills to auto cast (ctrl + right click).

Opener is Spear 3 into Spear 2.

Cast Spear 3, Spear 2 after an auto attack if they are up.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity engineer build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic Conditions = 10 conditions on golem. This build scales with conditions on foe (modified ammunition).

0:00 Rotation
2:11 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, more or less the opener is Spear 3 into Spear 2, and then you want to use Spear 3 and Spear 2 if they are up after an auto attack. I don't play this class very much, so I don't have a really strong read on the spear auto, so I'm sure the number in the video can probably be improved upon. The damage is already going to be good, though.

One thing you want to make sure of is that your mech skills are set on autocast. The way you do it is you hold Control and right-click all your mech skills.

For your notable spear skills, Spear 4 is a CC. Spear 5 can be used as a damage increase in the build, but it's also a mobility skill if that's something you wish to save it for.

For notable utilities, Healing Turret is an AoE heal and condition cleanse option for your team. Overclock Signet lets you respawn your mech, so you can save it to respawn your mech if it dies for whatever reason. Shift Signet you probably want to save unless it's absolutely necessary, but it is a blink. Force Signet can be used as extra CC. Superconducting Signet you also want to save. You can technically use it as a DPS increase, but you would want to switch your grandmaster trait so the signets keep providing their passive bonuses. If you want to play it the way I presented, then your autoattack is going to be your best option. If you want to swap out utility skills, you would probably swap Force Signet in this case. Barrier Signet is a projectile block. Rocket Boots is extra mobility. Personal Battering Ram is extra CC. Throw Mine is a CC skill and a boon strip skill.

Engineer also has a lot of kits, which I kind of look at as weapon-swap-ish, so I don't use them in these builds. Those are some of the more notable ones. Thank you for watching and I hope you all enjoy the build.
